title: BITSAT 2020
question: In the following question find the odd letters/group from the given alternatives.
solution:
As, $$B\buildrel { - 1} \over \longrightarrow A\buildrel { + 3} \over \longrightarrow D\buildrel { - 1} \over \longrightarrow C$$

$$J\buildrel { - 1} \over \longrightarrow I\buildrel { + 3} \over \longrightarrow L\buildrel { - 1} \over \longrightarrow K$$

$$N\buildrel { - 1} \over \longrightarrow M\buildrel { + 3} \over \longrightarrow P\buildrel { - 1} \over \longrightarrow O$$

But, $$V\buildrel { - 1} \over \longrightarrow U\buildrel { + 2} \over \longrightarrow W\buildrel { + 1} \over \longrightarrow X$$

Except option (d), all others follow same pattern ($$-$$1, +3, $$-$$1).

title: BITSAT 2023
question: Four options have been given, out of which three are alike in some manner and one is different. Select the option that is different.
options:
Delhi
Puducherry
Chandigarh
Jammu and Kashmir
solution:
Chandigarh is the capital of two states Haryana and Punjab.

$$\therefore$$ Chandigarh is different from others.

title: BITSAT 2024
question: Four options have been given, out of which three are alike in some manners and one is different. Select the option that is different.
solution:
The answer is Option C: Pigeon.

Here's why:

The cassowary, emu, and ostrich are all large, flightless birds.

The pigeon, on the other hand, is a bird that can fly and is much smaller.

Thus, the pigeon is the one that is different from the others.

title: BITSAT 2025
question: Four letter-clusters have been given, out of which three are alike in some manner and one is different. Select the odd letter-cluster.
solution:
As per the options,

(a) $S \xrightarrow{-1} R \xrightarrow{+5} W \xrightarrow{+1} X$

(b) $\mathrm{H}^{-1} \mathrm{G} \xrightarrow{+5} \mathrm{~L} \xrightarrow{+1} \mathrm{M}$

(c) $\mathrm{P} \xrightarrow{-1} \mathrm{O} \xrightarrow{+5} \mathrm{~T} \xrightarrow{+1} \mathrm{U}$
